_comment: Layer 3 MULTI-LEVEL subagent fan-out (spawn depth 2). Like examples/web_research_fanout.json but with enough sub-questions (6) to justify a COORDINATOR tier: the LEAD may spawn 2-3 ISOLATED coordinator sub-agents, each owning a SLICE of the sub-questions and itself using sessions_spawn/sessions_yield to delegate to leaf workers (LEAD -> COORDINATOR -> leaf) so EVERY level stays context-bounded (no agent holds all the raw pages). The depth knob lives in the CONFIG json, NOT here: set openclaw.fanout.max_spawn_depth to 2 in the config (the remote applies it via `openclaw config set agents.defaults.subagents.maxSpawnDepth 2` during bootstrap, and appends a depth-aware clause to the lead message). With depth 1 (default) the same task still runs, just flat (lead spawns all 6 leaf workers itself). timeout_seconds is SHORT on purpose: the synthesis lands quickly but `openclaw agent --local --json` then HANGS until killed while subagent children are reaped, so the remote recovers the lead synthesis from the server-side trajectory (_lead_synthesis_from_trajectory) and judges success on captured text, not the CLI returncode. Run on L4 (capable model drives multi-level orchestration): --gpu L4 --config configs/diffusiongemma_research.json (add openclaw.fanout.max_spawn_depth=2) --task examples/web_research_fanout_deep.json. BRAVE_API_KEY forwarded from ~/.env.
topic: Latest stable versions across language/runtime ecosystems (multi-level fan-out, depth 2)
steps: the current latest stable version of Python (from python.org)
the current latest LTS version of Node.js (from nodejs.org)
the current latest stable version of Go (from go.dev)
the current latest stable version of Rust (from rust-lang.org)
the current latest stable version of Ruby (from ruby-lang.org)
the current latest LTS version of the Java Development Kit (from oracle.com or adoptium.net)
